subject: Deep legal architecture spec: two-token model + foundation-as-escrow for Marshall Islands Series DAO LLC
description:
The Architect has laid out the full legal architecture. Capture EVERYTHING below into a detailed spec document at rigs/entity-legal/research/legal-architecture.md:

## Product Scope
- ONLY two products: Nonprofit Series DAO LLC and For-Profit Series DAO LLC (both Marshall Islands)
- On-chain scaffolding created per entity + series LLC registered under master LLC

## Email-Based Wallet (Privy Alternative)
- Email login creates a wallet behind the scenes — NO seed phrases, NO key management exposed to user
- Recovery via email (like Privy but self-hosted, no Privy dependency)
- Build on Solana using PDAs + program-owned accounts (NOT EVM account abstraction)
- User never sees keys — just email + dashboard

## Two-Token Model (Critical Innovation)
- Each LLC has TWO tokens:
  1. SECURITY TOKEN: Under Marshall Islands law, allows owning up to 25% as informal holder ANONYMOUSLY (no KYC needed for <25%)
  2. UTILITY TOKEN: Has governance rights, can vote on company decisions (Marshall Islands law allows this for utility tokens)
- Users can SWAP between utility and security tokens
- Security token swap REQUIRES KYC
- This solves the ownership/anonymity/compliance trilemma

## Foundation Structure (The Key Innovation)
- A FOUNDATION acts as representative of each LLC (not director — representative)
- Foundation OWNS all shares of the LLC
- Foundation facilitates the swap between utility and security tokens
- Foundation performs KYC for security token swaps (acts as escrow)
- Foundation provides legal shielding — not a natural person as director
- The program (smart contract) is autonomous under Marshall Islands law

## The Killer Use Case
- AI agents can create a company via API, get a tax ID
- Agent owns 100% technically (through the foundation) without triggering KYC
- Foundation is representative, not director — legal shielding
- Only jurisdiction where AI can own a company

## Research Needed
1. Marshall Islands Revised Uniform Limited Liability Company Act — full Series LLC provisions
2. Marshall Islands DAO LLC Act (2022) — exact text on smart contract as authoritative registry
3. 25% informal holder threshold — exact statute reference
4. Utility token governance rights under Marshall Islands law — statutory basis
5. Foundation as representative vs director — legal distinction and shielding
6. Tax ID issuance process for Series LLCs
7. Non-profit vs for-profit Series DAO LLC differences under Marshall Islands law
8. KYC/AML requirements for security token swaps

Create the spec, then update the landing page copy to reflect this architecture. The narrative: 'The first legal entity designed for agents, not humans.'
